question: Will real GDP increase by more than 2.0% in Q1 2026?
reasoning: The Atlanta Fed's GDPNow model, the most closely watched nowcast for Q1 2026 GDP, has been steadily declining from 3.1% in February to just 1.3% as of April 7, 2026. This dramatic decline from well above 2.0% to significantly below suggests weak economic data has been accumulating throughout the quarter. The model's latest estimate of 1.3% is well below the 2.0% threshold needed for the market to resolve YES.
key_factors: Atlanta Fed GDPNow estimate has fallen to 1.3% as of April 7, 2026
Model has shown consistent downward revisions from 3.1% in February
Q4 2025 GDP growth was only 0.7% (revised down from 1.4%), creating negative momentum
Advance estimate is due April 30, 2026 - just 3 weeks away
Economic data throughout Q1 has been weaker than expected
risks: GDPNow model has margin of error of ~1.17 percentage points historically
Final two weeks of data releases could potentially revise estimates upward
Model is not perfect - Q4 2025 advance estimate was much lower than GDPNow predicted
Potential for measurement revisions or methodology changes in BEA calculation
Geopolitical events or policy changes could impact final calculations
